sources.exact_document_title: Guidelines for the investigation of chronic diarrhoea in adults: British Society of Gastroenterology, 3rd edition
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international gastroenterology evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Adults with chronic diarrhoea; paediatric and acute infectious presentations require separate evidence.
sources.applicability_note: Supports duration, stool characteristics, alarm features, medicine, surgery, diet, travel, family, examination, and clinician-reviewed investigation context without diagnosing a cause or ordering tests.

exact_sections.heading: Clinical assessment — history and examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and physical examination.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations for excluding cancer, inflammation, malabsorption, and common disorders
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of existing blood, stool, FIT, endoscopy, imaging, coeliac, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption investigations.

archive.title: Guidelines for the investigation of chronic diarrhoea in adults: British Society of Gastroenterology, 3rd edition
archive.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international gastroenterology evidence requiring UAE adaptation
archive.population: Adults with chronic diarrhoea; paediatric and acute infectious presentations require separate evidence.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Clinical assessment — history and examination
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports duration, stool pattern, urgency, incontinence, weight loss, nocturnal symptoms, medicines, surgery, travel, family history, diet, and physical examination.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations for excluding cancer, inflammation, malabsorption, and common disorders
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of existing blood, stool, FIT, endoscopy, imaging, coeliac, bile-acid, microscopic-colitis, and lactose-malabsorption investigations.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth gastroenteritis documentation; partial for a broad diarrhea workflow without an adult or acute-duration filter.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — clinical manifestations and history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Investigation/Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.
